title: Premier League results
description: CRICKET NEWS - Union Stars I, Pacaltsdorp...
bodyText:
CRICKET NEWS - Union Stars I, Pacaltsdorp United and Knysna Cavaliers recorded victories during the Six Gun Grill SWD Premier League fixtures played on 25 and 26 January.


> At New Horizons, the defending champions, Pacaltsdorp United (255/9), posted a competitive total with all batters contributed and thereafter dismissed Plett Pioneers for only 117 for a 138-run bonus point victory.


Union Stars I (148 all out) consolidated their position at the top of the table with a 66-run bonus point victory against George (82 all out) at Rooirivierrif. The leg-spinner, Regan Rhode (5/12 in 8 overs), was in outstanding form to secure Union Stars a victory away from home.

Knysna Cavaliers (141/6) was victorious against Union Stars II (140 all out) in a clash at the Bridgton Sport Grounds.

The Garden Route Badgers u.19 captain, Josef Enslin (71), played a match-winning innings for the visitors to enable them a bonus point four-wicket victory.

Harlequins Forces, following a contravention of the administrative conditions, was suspended from all league competitions – including the premier league.


> The summarised score cards of the weekend are:


Knysna Cavaliers beat Union Stars II by 4 wickets:

Union Stars II 140 all out - 31.3 overs (Breyton Arries 64*, Elridge Booysen 26, Shannon Opperman 22, Lutho Ngqongwa 2/11)

Knysna Cavaliers 141/6 - 24.3 overs (Joof Enslin 71, Jonathan Bester 28, Adriaan van der Merwe 4/41, Shannon Opperman 2/26)

Pacaltsdorp United beat Plett Pioneers by 138 runs:

Pacaltsdorp United 255/9 - 50 overs (Warren Bell 39, Elih Thorne 37, Grant Simpson 36*, Matthew Thorne 32, Leroi Bredenkamp 29, Carlos Koeberg 4/42, Sinovuyo Dali 2/46)

Plett Pioneers 117 all out - 33 overs (John Koopman 35, George van Heerden 2/5, Elih Thorne 2/14, Ricardo Majolla 2/31)

Union Stars I beat George by 66 runs:

Union Stars I 148 - 43.2 overs (Breyton Plaatjies 36, Erinn Ewerts 27, Jarren Bacher 2/21, Shelby Lindeque 2/24)

George 82 all out - 24.3 overs (Kelly Smuts 32, Regan Rhode 5/12, Yamkela Xakani 2/4)

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
